summary: The DSVW repository scored 84/100 (Grade F), indicating critical security risks across multiple attack surfaces. The analysis identified 18 vulnerabilities including 4 critical and 6 high severity findings. The most severe issues are SQL injection and command injection vulnerabilities in the main application file, which are directly exploitable without authentication. Additionally, the application intentionally disables all security controls as a teaching tool, making every common web vulnerability present and exploitable. Immediate remediation would require a complete architectural redesign rather than targeted patches.

findings.title: SQL Injection via Unsanitised User Input
findings.description: User input is concatenated directly into a SQL query string with no parameterisation. An attacker can manipulate the query to dump the entire database, bypass authentication, or in some configurations execute OS commands via xp_cmdshell.
findings.code_snippet: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=%s" % req_params['id'])
findings.patch_explanation: Use parameterised queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=?', (req_params['id'],))

exploit_path.title: Database Dump via UNION-Based SQL Injection
prerequisites: Knowledge of table names (obtainable via error messages)

steps.action: Send crafted GET request with UNION payload
steps.result: Application returns all usernames and hashed passwords

exploit_path.impact: Full database exfiltration. All user credentials exposed.
exploit_path.proof_of_concept:
import requests
url = 'http://target/?id=1 UNION SELECT username,password,3 FROM users--'
resp = requests.get(url)
print(resp.text)

findings.title: OS Command Injection via Shell=True Subprocess
findings.description: User-supplied input is passed directly to subprocess.Popen with shell=True. An attacker can append arbitrary shell commands using semicolons or pipes, leading to full remote code execution on the server.
findings.patch_explanation: Never pass user input to shell=True. Use a whitelist of allowed commands and pass as a list: subprocess.Popen(['allowed_cmd'], shell=False)

findings.title: Cross-Site Scripting (Reflected XSS)
findings.description: User input is reflected in the HTTP response without HTML encoding. An attacker can inject script tags that execute in the victim's browser, enabling session theft, keylogging, or phishing.
findings.patch_explanation: HTML-encode all user input before reflecting it: html.escape(req_params['name'])

findings.title: Path Traversal — Arbitrary File Read
findings.description: The file parameter is not sanitised before being used to open a file. An attacker can use ../ sequences to read arbitrary files outside the web root, including /etc/passwd, application configs, and private keys.
findings.patch_explanation: Resolve the path and verify it is within the allowed directory: os.path.realpath(path).startswith(allowed_dir)

findings.title: Outdated Dependency: Werkzeug 0.9.6 (CVE-2023-25577)
findings.description: Werkzeug 0.9.6 is affected by CVE-2023-25577, a multipart form parsing vulnerability that allows denial of service via crafted requests. The current version is 3.0+.
